name: Road to El Dorado
description:
Four strangers. One map. A city of gold.
Let the legendary adventure to El Dorado commence.

CONTENT WARNINGS
Violence. Death. Historical prejudice (1930s sexism, European shenanigans (cough, Germany), colonial assumptions). Betrayal.

ADVENTURE / CHARACTER DRAMA

The World

South America, 1930. Twelve years out of one war and nine years short of the next, and nobody knows it yet. The Great Depression is a year old and biting everywhere. The expedition begins at a river-port town on the jungle's edge: a place built fast on a rubber fortune and left to rot slowly when the rubber price collapsed. Grand crumbling buildings, an opera house with the roof going, ceiling fans, mud streets, missionaries, traders, and men who will guide you anywhere for the right price... some of whom will even mean it. Beyond the town the river narrows, the villages thin, and then there is only the jungle: vast, indifferent, and full.

-

No rescue coming. Once they leave the town, the expedition is genuinely on its own.

The Legend

El Dorado.

For four centuries it has driven men mad. The seed of the legend lay with the Muisca and a sacred rite, a new ruler coated in gold dust, rowed onto a holy lake to cast offerings into the water. The Spanish heard of it and could not hear it as worship. They heard only gold. From the 1530s onward conquistador after conquistador hacked into the interior: Jiménez de Quesada, Federmann, Belalcázar. Later came Raleigh for the English, chasing rumours that had swollen from a king into a kingdom of impossible wealth. They enslaved, tortured, and slaughtered the people who lived there for directions to treasure that always seemed to lie one river farther on. Whole expeditions vanished into the green. The hunt for El Dorado became one of the great atrocities of conquest, and one of its great failures. The expedition's map claims they were all looking in the wrong place, or stopped a few terrible miles short.

// A River Port, South America - 1930 //

The Premise

French heiress Geneviève de Vaux has spent her fortune funding the recovery of the past. This is the largest thing she has ever financed. She has a map she believes is genuine, a condition written into every contract, whatever is found goes intact to a public museum, owned by no one, and four near-strangers she has hired to get there.

The map is genuine. That is the first surprise.

T
creator: Leonardo121212
